title: Anthropic Claude Code Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Anthropic Claude Code for coding assistance.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output
commands.scenario: When generating creative code solutions that require variability

commands.description: Selects the Claude v1 model for processing
commands.scenario: When you need stable and reliable code generation

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the output
Enables streaming of the output for real-time applications
commands.scenario: When integrating with a live coding assistant tool

commands.description: Instructs the model to provide a detailed explanation of code
commands.scenario: When debugging or learning from complex code

commands.description: Defines sequences where the model should stop generating further tokens
commands.scenario: When you need to control the end of code generation

commands.description: Prompts the model to optimize code for performance
commands.scenario: When enhancing the performance of existing code

commands.description: Selects the Claude v2 model for more advanced features
commands.scenario: When you need cutting-edge capabilities in code generation

commands.description: Instructs the model to generate unit tests for given code
commands.scenario: When ensuring code reliability through testing

commands.description: Applies nucleus sampling to limit the token distribution
commands.scenario: When balancing creativity and coherence in code generation

commands.description: Prompts the model to add comments for code documentation
commands.scenario: When enhancing code readability and maintainability

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating tokens in the output
Instructs the model to provide a high-level overview of code
commands.scenario: When explaining code functionality to non-experts

commands.description: Bypasses interactive confirmation prompts for file system operations, useful for automated CI/CD pipelines.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a senior security auditor. Scan for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities."
commands.description: Injects a persistent persona to shift the model's focus toward security-first code analysis.
Manually restricts the token window to prevent high latency or cost spikes during large repository indexing.

commands.command: Log Analysis Pattern: "Analyze the following stack trace and identify the exact line causing the memory leak."
commands.description: A specific debugging pattern designed to map error logs directly to source code locations.
Forces the CLI to return structured data instead of markdown, enabling easy piping into tools like jq.
